message:
So I have this friend who texts me every time there's a storm watch—full panic mode, ALL CAPS, "WHAT DO WE DO?!" I used to find it kind of funny (okay, I still do a little), but I realized last year that underneath the drama, she was genuinely stressed because she actually *didn't know* where to start.

We spent like 20 minutes one afternoon just talking through the basics: where to keep supplies, how to check her phone for alerts, what "preparedness" actually looks like (spoiler: it's not glamorous, mostly just having stuff in your house). Now when a storm comes, she still texts me—but it's more like "I'm good, got my flashlights" instead of full meltdown.

The thing is, a little prep *really does* quiet that background panic. Not because you're obsessed with worst-case scenarios, but because you just... know what you'd do. No chaos, no last-minute scrambling.

Does anyone else have a friend (or are you the friend 😄) who gets in a tizzy when weather turns sketchy?
